---
title: "Topics"
page-layout: full
toc: false
number-sections: false
---

## Why Topics Are The Main Spine

Topics are organized by mathematical object and use, not by textbook.

Use `Topics` when the main question is still:

- what is the object here
- what assumptions and proof patterns carry it
- what first-pass order should I actually use

Every mature topic is meant to connect:

- concept
- proof pattern
- exercises or practice
- applications
- paper bridge
- research direction

## Choose Your Branch

::: {.card-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### Foundation

Start here when algebra, proofs, calculus, probability, or statistics still feel uneven.

[Open Foundation Topics](foundation/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Advanced

Open this branch when the foundations already feel usable and you want theory, systems, numerics, or research-facing math.

[Open Advanced Topics](advanced/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Need a Study Order?

If sequencing is the main blocker, use a roadmap first and come back into the topic tree with a clearer route.

[Open Roadmaps](../roadmaps/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::
:::

## Best First Routes

- If you need the strongest repair-first entry, start with [Core Foundation Roadmap](../roadmaps/core-foundation.qmd).
- If you want the shortest paper-reading math stack, start with [Proofs](foundation/proofs/index.qmd), [Linear Algebra](foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd), and [Probability](foundation/probability/index.qmd).
- If you care about systems and simulation, move from [Multivariable Calculus](foundation/multivariable-calculus/index.qmd) into [ODEs and Dynamical Systems](advanced/odes-and-dynamical-systems/index.qmd) and [Numerical Methods](advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd).
